summary: Both retirements are genuine ZFC cleanups. R1 removes the PreToolUse heuristic command-string matcher (matchMindspecComplete/runPreComplete + the commandSegments tokenizer that string-scanned a free-form, agent-writable shell command to guess `mindspec complete` and extract a bead ID) and the pre-complete hook wiring (dispatch arm, Names entry, setup install). R2 removes the prose path-extractor extractMentionedPaths (scraped file paths from plan prose by extension) and its PlanFidelity call site. Both DELETE framework string-guessing and introduce NO replacement heuristic: the spec explicitly records that any thin non-heuristic PreToolUse backstop is impossible without re-introducing the ZFC violation (option (a) full retire), and R2 takes option (b) retire over a structured replacement. Verified via diff that no new classifier/guesser is added. ADR-0037 2026-06-17 amendment is accurate: it records the deferred retirement (prior text 'retained but non-authoritative, retirement deferred') as LANDED, keeps Status: Accepted (amended on top, prior amendments preserved per 099/100/101 pattern), correctly names the in-binary `mindspec complete` gate over internal/panel (PanelGateDecision/ResolveGateFacts/gate.go) as the SOLE authoritative enforcement point, and annotates the stale Validation/Rollout Bead-4 scenario enumeration as Retired. Every symbol cited by the amendment is real (internal/panel/gate.go, ResolveGateFacts, PanelGateDecision, the TestPanelGate_* suite incl. sub-threshold/staleness/fail-open/hatch tests intact in internal/complete). No contradiction: only ADR-0037 is cited+amended; ADR-0036 (the ZFC anchor) is correctly UNCHANGED because the diff conforms to its stance (removes guessing) rather than contradicting it. Coverage holds: ADR-0037 (workflow,execution) covers both domains, ADR-0036 (workflow) is the ZFC anchor. Build clean (go build ./...), and hook/setup/complete/panel packages pass. NON-BLOCKING NOTE (pre-existing, outside diff): internal/instruct/panelstate.go:180 still emits 'This INFORMS; the pre-complete hook ENFORCES.' in --panel-state output, now stale since the pre-complete hook is retired and `mindspec complete` enforces; worth a follow-up bead but does not block this spec (the file is untouched by 102 and the misstatement is cosmetic instruct prose, not a gate behavior).
